examples: Line 11: 'The city had been cut into tasks and was being finished as tasks' -> 'The city had been cut into sections and was being finished one section at a time' to remove planner-register drag and restore lived narration.
Line 295: cut 'because the habit had become automatic' so the face-reading habit stays implied instead of explained.
observations: Lines 5-17: the opening already matched the style target's dry, controlled aftermath register; Stratholme is rendered through bells, carts, soot, flour, and bread before theory takes over.
Line 11 carried a faint planning-note cadence; fixing 'tasks' to 'sections' kept Jaina's analytical lens without sounding like process language.
Lines 89-97: Sylvanas's 'South' / 'We go south' lands in exactly the clipped, smoke-rough register her voice sheet promises.
Lines 163-215: Antonidas and Jaina sound like teacher and prodigy under strain rather than generic fantasy mourners; the scene stays precise instead of grandiose.
Lines 363-371: the closing keeps the chapter in exhausted silence, not purple prophecy; it sounds like this book's endgame lane, not an editorial flourish.

examples: Line 295: removed explanatory behavioral gloss after 'Jaina watched the ranger's face' so the paragraph stays in immediate perception.
Line 361: 'The motion reached Jaina as surely as touch' -> 'Jaina felt it as surely as touch' to restore POV ownership of the sensation.
observations: Lines 33-34: Sylvanas remains strictly external; Jaina reads stance, balance, and the unstrung bow, not hidden feeling.
Lines 69-77: the self-comparison to Kel'Thuzad and Arthas stays framed as Jaina's fear about her own habits, not narrator verdict.
Lines 109-119: Cyndia is read through visible competence—horses, water, tack, spacing—not interior motive.
Line 295 had the last obvious POV-distance clause; trimming it let the face read carry itself.
Line 361 had mild inanimate-agency seep; fixing it keeps the final northward beat fully inside Jaina's body.

notes: Chapter 17's wall witness carries straight into chapter 18 with warm stone, smoke, and Arthas below the wall still physically present.
The chapter treats Stratholme as familiar terrain now: Market Row and Alonsus Chapel return as callbacks, not as fresh exposition.
observations: Lines 3-21 honor the established Stratholme sensory ledger: warm parapet, soot, flour, bells gone silent, district smoke, bread wrongness.
Lines 39-57 advance the clean-hands image from chapter17 without reintroducing Arthas from scratch; the memory work deepens the wound instead of resetting it.
Lines 109-121 preserve the repaired Jaina/Sylvanas/Cyndia field formation; no old verification pause is reintroduced.
Lines 163-255 keep Antonidas as a live mentor tether from chapter14, not a surprise rescue line or emotional reset.
Lines 301-371 lock the chapter19 handoff facts correctly: Arthas north, Uther west, south as present road, north as the real pressure, with no premature turn and no forbidden Frostmourne naming.

notes: Multiple short-sentence clusters were reviewed and retained because they serve shock inventory, stair-step movement, and closing pressure rather than creating monotone.
No rhythm edit was needed once the line-11 diction fix cleaned the one place where cadence and register both dragged.
observations: Lines 7-17 use clipped inventory well: city hum -> fire lines -> soot and flour. The short runs sound like stunned witness, not lazy fragmentation.
Lines 39-57 vary cleanly between hard fragments ('Arthas's hands.') and longer memory sentences, which keeps the personal-history beat from flattening.
Lines 69-79 build with medium-length reasoning, then puncture it with blunt shocks—'And she—' / nail in stone / 'The thought itself was obscene.'
Lines 103-121 give the descent good bodily rhythm: stair, yard, water, mounting, formation. The sequence never loses motion under reflection.
Lines 333-371 let the repeated 'North' beats act as deliberate drum hits; the surrounding prose is varied enough that the repetition reads as pressure, not chant.

continuity.crossChapter: Chapter 17 -> Chapter 18 coherence holds: same wall state, same smoke, same clean-hands aftermath, then a causal descent into motion and the unspoken north question for chapter19.
observations: The opening acknowledges chapter17 exactly where it left off: Arthas already below the wall, the city still being worked district by district, Jaina still physically at the parapet.
The wall-to-stair transition is causal, not temporal: Sylvanas's hand and 'South' move Jaina bodily off the wall.
The stair-to-yard-to-road run preserves spatial logic all the way through horses, tack, formation, and the south road.
Antonidas's sending-stone interruption grows naturally out of Sylvanas's 'Stone' cue and Jaina's notebook work; no conversational seam shows.
The relay rider's report lands as external confirmation of the model Jaina already has, which makes the north pivot feel earned instead of tacked on.

notes: No leftover scene-opening cliché: the chapter opens on heated stone and a silenced city, not on waking, weather, or neutral recap.
No biography reinsertions or explanatory clauses survived around Arthas, Stratholme, or Antonidas beyond what the current scene needs.
observations: Lines 3-31 keep aftermath grounded in stone, soot, flour, bells, and residue rather than abstract grief-summary.
Lines 41-57 sharpen the clean-hands witness without adding moral footnotes; the horror lands through image and memory instead of editorializing.
Lines 123-153 keep the road section free of dead-air travel filler; every travel beat either advances the model or carries bodily aftermath.
Lines 189-255 trust the Antonidas exchange without over-tagging or glossing each emotional beat; the silence does real work.
Lines 339-371 end on objects and pressure—dispatch tube, notebook, salt wind, warm stone in the bag—rather than on explanatory thesis.

notes: No banned bureaucratic or clinical vocabulary appeared in spoken lines.
Jaina's higher-register plague language is reserved for Antonidas, which matches both the voice sheet and the style-guide audience filter.
observations: Lines 91-95: Sylvanas's speech stays plain, brief, and imperative.
Lines 171-189: Jaina's first answers to Antonidas sound tired and young in the right way; the scene does not let her slip into report voice.
Line 209 keeps 'relay' because she is speaking to an archmage mentor about a specific plague mechanism; the term reads as earned technical shorthand, not document contamination.
Lines 297-317: Cyndia and Sylvanas keep the relay exchange field-plain—'Say it,' 'Bound for Northrend,' 'Uther rides west'—with no bureaucratic padding.
Lines 341-343: 'Camp another mile south. Rotate the relay horse out. She eats before she reports again.' sounds like Sylvanas giving orders, not a system summary.
